metadata.subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update

risks.description: Confirmed 2-week delay in delivery of Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches pushes arrival to March 20, after the March 8 migration window opens, making it impossible to meet the March 6 staging/testing deadline and likely causing the Q2 migration milestone to slip.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: Single-source dependency on TechSupplier's Montreal warehouse for the Cisco Catalyst 9500 product line means there is no rapid alternative supply path; the only fallback (Vancouver depot) adds a further 3–5 business days, deepening the delay rather than mitigating it.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse, and alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: The root cause of the delay is a warehouse management system (WMS) failure at the supplier's primary distribution centre, which is an ongoing IT outage of unknown resolution timeline; if the WMS is not restored promptly, the March 20 revised date is itself at risk of further slippage.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments since February 26.

risks.description: Cascading resource and cost risk: SSC technical staff, contractors, and the Gatineau facility migration window are likely pre-booked around the March 8 start date. A forced reschedule may incur idle labour costs, change-order fees, and potential contractual penalties if the Q2 migration commitment is tied to government service-level obligations.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: Implicit compliance and governance risk: NGIS Phase 3 is a Government of Canada shared-services infrastructure program likely subject to Treasury Board project gating and departmental service continuity requirements. A delayed migration may breach committed service transition dates, triggering mandatory reporting, ministerial briefings, or audit scrutiny.
affected_stakeholders: client departments dependent on NGIS infrastructure

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: Confirmed 2-week delay in delivery of Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches pushes arrival to March 20, after the March 8 migration window opens, directly threatening the Q2 migration schedule.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: Single-source dependency on TechSupplier's Montreal warehouse for the Cisco Catalyst 9500 product line means there is no rapid alternative supply path; the only fallback (Vancouver depot) adds a further 3–5 business days, deepening the delay risk.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse, and alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: The root cause of the delay is a warehouse management system (WMS) failure at the supplier's distribution centre. The failure is ongoing and its full resolution timeline is unconfirmed, creating risk of further slippage beyond March 20.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments since February 26.

risks.description: Staging and testing activities requiring the equipment on-site by March 6 cannot proceed as planned, risking compressed or skipped pre-migration validation, which could introduce undetected technical failures into the data centre migration.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our migration window opens March 8 and we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: Cascading budget risk: the schedule disruption may trigger costs associated with extended resource bookings, idle contractor or staff time during the delayed period, and potential contractual penalties or re-procurement costs if the Q2 milestone is missed.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

metadata.subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update

risks.description: Confirmed 2-week delay in delivery of Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches pushes arrival to March 20, after the March 8 migration window opens, making the scheduled migration start impossible without equipment on site for staging and testing.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: Single-source dependency on TechSupplier's Montreal warehouse for the Cisco Catalyst 9500 product line means there is no rapid alternative supply path; the only fallback (Vancouver depot) adds a further 3–5 business days, deepening the delay rather than mitigating it.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse, and alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: The root cause of the delay is a warehouse management system (WMS) failure at TechSupplier's primary distribution centre, which is an ongoing IT outage of unknown resolution timeline. If the WMS issue is not resolved promptly, the March 20 revised date is itself at risk of further slippage.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments since February 26.

risks.description: The Q2 migration timeline for the NGIS Phase 3 data centre migration is under significant pressure. A slip past March 20 (or inability to complete staging and testing before the migration window) risks missing Q2 commitments, with potential downstream budget impacts such as extended legacy infrastructure costs or contract penalties.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: The staging and testing window between equipment arrival and migration start has been eliminated or severely compressed. Even if equipment arrives March 20, there is no buffer for defective units, configuration issues, or failed acceptance testing before the migration window, risking a go-live with untested infrastructure.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our migration window opens March 8 and we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: Critical dependency on Catalyst 9500 network switch delivery creates a hard blocker for final DC network connectivity testing. If switches are delayed beyond approximately March 14, the four-day buffer before the March 18 migration window is consumed, making the migration date unachievable.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The only dependency is the network switch delivery — we need the Catalyst 9500s in place before we can do final network connectivity testing for the DCs.

risks.description: The production DC staging window (targeting March 14) leaves only four days of buffer before the March 18 migration. Any slippage in staging, unforeseen patching issues, or GPO migration errors in production will eliminate all schedule contingency with no recovery time built in.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I will begin the production DC staging activities next week, targeting completion by March 14 to give us four days of buffer before the March 18 migration window.

risks.description: The AD schema extension has only been validated in a test environment and is scheduled for promotion to staging this weekend — not yet validated in a production-equivalent environment. Schema changes are irreversible in Active Directory; any undiscovered conflicts in staging or production could corrupt the directory and require full rollback.
affected_stakeholders: All AD-dependent users across Sites A, B, and C

evidence_refs.excerpt: The AD schema extension ran successfully in the test environment last night. No errors or attribute conflicts detected. Ready to promote to staging this weekend.

risks.description: 24 unique GPOs migrated from Sites B and C may contain environment-specific settings that behave differently when applied at Site A, potentially causing security policy misconfigurations or service disruptions post-migration. Security review was performed by a single reviewer (Dave Chen), introducing a single-point-of-failure in the compliance validation process.

evidence_refs.excerpt: 24 unique GPOs will be migrated. The migration script is ready and has been reviewed by Dave Chen for security policy compliance.

risks.description: The rollback procedure has only been tested in a lab environment, not under production load or network conditions. The 45-minute rollback estimate may be optimistic in a live scenario involving real replication latency, active user sessions, and production data volumes, leaving the organization exposed to extended outage if Go-Live fails.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Rollback procedure tested successfully in lab — estimated rollback time is 45 minutes from Go-Live.

metadata.subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update

risks.description: Confirmed 2-week delay in delivery of Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches pushes arrival to March 20, after the March 8 migration window opens, directly threatening the Q2 migration schedule.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: Single-source dependency on TechSupplier's Montreal warehouse for the Cisco Catalyst 9500 product line creates a critical supply chain vulnerability with no rapid fallback; the only alternative (Vancouver depot) adds a further 3–5 business days of delay.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse, and alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: Staging and testing window is eliminated: Alice's email specifies equipment must arrive by March 6 for staging and testing before the March 8 migration window, meaning even the confirmed March 20 delivery leaves zero time for pre-migration validation, increasing the risk of a failed or defective deployment.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our migration window opens March 8 and we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: The supplier's warehouse management system failure is an unresolved root cause; if the WMS issue persists or recurs, the March 20 revised delivery date is itself unreliable, risking further cascading delays beyond what is currently communicated.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments since February 26.

risks.description: Q2 budget and contractual commitments are implicitly at risk: a delayed migration may trigger contract penalty clauses, force resource re-booking costs, or cause SSC to miss government fiscal-year spending authorities tied to the Q2 milestone.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.
